Rhodiola (Rhodiola rosea) is a perennial plant in the family Crassulaceae, native to the cold, high-altitude regions of the world, including Tibet.

The health benefits of Rhodiola include reducing stress and anxiety, improving mental performance and memory, and enhancing mood. It has also been shown to have adaptogenic properties, helping the body to adapt to physical and mental stress.

The bioactive constituents of Rhodiola include rosavin, salidroside, and tyrosol, which are responsible for its adaptogenic and antioxidant properties.

The most common herbal preparations made from Rhodiola include capsules, tablets, and teas, which are often used to support mental health and well-being.
